note: Definitions are independently authored football meanings. The generator must consume only definition text, not concept ids, gold chains, catalog refs, or known provider paths.

definitions.text: A give-and-go is a two-pass combination where the first passer plays to a teammate and then receives the return pass back as the terminal receiver.
A wall-pass sequence has the ball leave the original passer, get relayed by a teammate, and come back to that same original passer.

perturbations.text: A two-pass sequence where the first passer plays to a teammate and the teammate continues the ball onward to a different terminal receiver.

definitions.text: Receive under pressure means the ball receiver is under observed defender pressure at the reception moment.
A reception under pressure occurs when the player receiving the ball has nearest-defender pressure at the receiving frame.
Carry out of pressure means the same carrier starts a carry under pressure and ends the carry with pressure reduced.
A player carries the ball from pressure into less pressure when the pressure distance improves between carry start and carry end.
A carry under defender pressure that also progresses forward.
A player carries the ball forward while under nearest-defender pressure.
Shape expansion means team shape width or depth increases across the same anchor after the observed moment.
A team-shape expansion occurs when the measured team width or depth gets larger after the anchor than before it.
Expected pass completion is the model probability that a pass will be completed.
Pass completion expectation means a learned likelihood or probability of a completed pass.
Goalkeeper set position proxy depends on the goalkeeper's body orientation and stance before the action.
A keeper set-position proxy asks whether the goalkeeper is facing and set in a particular stance.
Goal-kick build structure means the team shape and first build pattern from a goal kick restart.
Goal kick build-up structure describes the restart shape and build pattern after the goal kick is taken.
Defender arrival time means an observed defender can arrive at the target point within the declared arrival window.
Reachability to a target means the observed defender is able to reach that point inside the arrival time threshold.

perturbations.text: A target point is described without asking whether any defender can get there in time.

definitions.text: Receiver reachability window means observed candidates can reach the receiving point within the arrival window.
A receiving target is reachable when observed players are able to arrive at the target point before the arrival time limit.
Lane occupation means observed outfield players occupy at least one lateral lane at the anchor.
Channel occupation occurs when observed outfield players are classified into an occupied pitch lane at the anchor.

perturbations.text: Observed outfield players are present at the anchor without any lateral grouping claim.

definitions.text: Lane classification means observed outfield players are classified into lateral lanes at the anchor.
Channel classification occurs when observed outfield players are assigned to pitch lanes at the anchor.
Covered lane reachability means observed outfield players occupy at least one lateral lane and observed defenders can arrive within the arrival window at the same anchor.
A channel is covered when outfield players occupy the pitch lanes and defenders are able to reach the target point inside the arrival time threshold.
Cover shadow region means the area behind a defender that the defender blocks or screens from the ball.
A cover shadow is a defended passing lane region hidden behind the pressing defender.
Passing-lane denial means a defender denies the passing lane between the ball and a potential receiver.
Lane denial occurs when a defender blocks the passing lane or cover shadow toward a target teammate.
Marking assignment means a defender is assigned as the marker of an attacking player.
A marker relation identifies which defender is marking a particular attacker.
A free player means an attacker is unmarked by their nearest marker.
An unmarked player candidate asks whether a receiver is not closely marked by a defender.
An off-ball run is a run by a player without the ball, tracked as a movement episode away from possession.
Off ball run typing identifies a player's run while they are away from the ball.
A run in behind is an off-ball run into space behind the defensive line.
Run-in-behind typing asks for an off ball run beyond the observed defensive line.
Acceleration burst means a player accelerates sharply over a short observed interval.
A burst of acceleration is rapid speeding up across consecutive frames.
Deceleration after receiving means the receiver slows down after the first touch.
A receiving deceleration occurs when the player is slowing down after the reception moment.
Corner variant candidate means a corner routine has a recognizable set-piece structure.
A corner variant is a set piece routine pattern used from the corner restart.
Set-piece attacking shape means the attackers form a structured set-piece arrangement before delivery.
Attacking shape at a set piece describes the routine structure of the attacking players.
Team press candidate means several defenders act as a collective press around the ball.
A team press is a collective defensive pressing structure rather than one nearest defender.
Pressing trap candidate means the defending team forms a pressing trap around a target area.
A pressing trap is a team press structure intended to constrain the ball carrier.
Open space region means a generated area of free space available to attack.
A free space region is an open space area produced by the current player positions.
Space creation candidate means an action creates generated space for a teammate.
Generated space creation asks whether a new free space region appears after the action.
